acceptanceSignals: CI fails when runtime HTTP routes drift from OpenAPI.
CI fails when generated API artifacts drift from the committed contract.
MCP contract checks are wired and automatically enforced once MCP endpoints/tools are present.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Contract Conformance CI in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The current feature satisfies the active policy: CI enforces contract conformance via the pull-request workflow gate (`cargo test --test openapi_contract_platform` plus `pnpm run contract:verify`), runtime HTTP route parity vs OpenAPI is asserted in tests, generated OpenAPI/client artifact drift is enforced by the drift check script against committed `contract/` outputs, and MCP contract checks are wired into the same gate with no legacy compatibility path retained for superseded contract locations.

excludedApproaches: Manual periodic contract review as the primary drift defense.
Allowing runtime behavior changes without synchronized contract updates.
Schema-only snapshot checks without behavior verification.
executionConstraints: Introduce merge-time contract conformance gates early in implementation.
Require synchronized updates to spec, generated artifacts, tests, and implementation.
Enable MCP parity checks in the same CI pipeline when `mcp-server-domain-parity` lands.
name: Contract Conformance CI
rationale: Early contract gates reduce architectural retries and integration churn.
summary: Establish automated contract drift prevention for the delivery stream.
